title: Paravallaris maingayi
intro: Paravallaris maingayi, also known as the Maingay's coral tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. Traditionally, Paravallaris maingayi has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its purported health benefits in lowering body temperature, reducing swelling, and alleviating gastrointestinal discomfort. The bioactive constituents of Paravallaris maingayi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Paravallaris maingayi is often prepared into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to treat a range of ailments, making it a valuable resource in folk medicine.
taxonomy: Paravallaris maingayi is a species of flowering plant in the family Dipterocarpaceae. This plant is native to Southeast Asia and is a part of the evergreen broad-leaved forest ecosystem. It is a type of tree that belongs to the order Malvales. The species name "maingayi" is often attributed to the person to whom the plant was dedicated, likely a botanist or collector. Further classification of Paravallaris maingayi would require more specific information on its subfamily or tribe.
